Asian Boyz.description: The Asian Boyz are a street gang based in Southern California, founded in the late 1980s by Cambodian refugees to protect themselves from other gangs. They have about 2,000 to 5,000 active members, predominantly Southeast Asian, and are involved in drug trafficking, robbery, and homicide. The gang is active in 28 cities across 14 U.S. states and has ties to the Crips.

edges.evidence_quote: One of the Asian gang that integrated with Long Beach Asian Boyz during the early 1990s was the Asian Boys Insanity (ABI).

Gas House Gang.description: The Gas House Gang was a New York City street gang active in the late 19th and early 20th centuries. Based in the Gas House district of Manhattan, it controlled the area along Third Avenue from 11th to 18th Street. The gang specialized in armed robbery, committing an estimated 30 to 40 robberies per night, and also engaged in extortion and operating brothels. It was eventually absorbed by the Five Points Gang in 1910.

descriptions: The Gas House Gang was absorbed by the Five Points Gang in 1910.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang continued to control the district for over two decades until it was eventually absorbed by the Five Points Gang in 1910.

descriptions: Members of Marginals and Gas House Gang were arrested together, suggesting cooperation or shared activity.
edges.evidence_quote: eight members of the Tanner Smiths were apprehended along with members of the Gas House and Car Barn Gangs in September 1910

descriptions: Brother Speed members attacked members of the Empties motorcycle club in 2012.
edges.evidence_quote: In May 2012, five of the Idaho Falls members of Brother Speed, attacked two members of Pocatello's Empties motorcycle club at a gathering for a child who was suffering from cancer.

Aava Gang.description: Sri Lankan motorcycle street gang active in the Northern Province, with about 60 members aged 18-25. The leader is nicknamed 'Haava' (rabbit in Sinhalese). The gang emerged after the Sri Lankan Civil War and is involved in drug trade, robbery, kidnapping, and violence. It gained national attention after attacking police officers in Chunnakam.

descriptions: Waray-Abuyog Gang has alliances with Sudoy-Sudoy Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: They have also been documented making alliances with other Waray gangs, most notably the Sudoy-Sudoy Gang.

descriptions: Pagans are affiliated with the Barbarians motorcycle gang.
edges.evidence_quote: The Pagan's have chapters in Charleston and Fairmont , and are affiliated with the Barbarians, a local motorcycle gang.

Mamak Gang.description: The Mamak Gang is a notorious Malaysian criminal organization active since the early 1990s. Initially specializing in breaking into factories and warehouses, they later shifted to carjacking and robbing motorists. They are most infamous for stealing RM 12 million worth of gold bars from the Malaysia Airlines cargo complex in 1994. Despite multiple arrests and preventive detention, members continued operations from prison until the gang's dissolution in December 2006.

descriptions: Daulat Ul-Islamiya is allied to Abu Sayyaf.
edges.evidence_quote: saying a group allied to them; the Daulat Ul-Islamiya were responsible.

Bullshit Motorcycle Club.description: Danish outlaw motorcycle gang and organized criminal group active during the 1980s. Formed in 1979 in Tårnby, Amager from a merger of Filthy Few and Nøragersmindebanden. Engaged in a violent turf war against the Hells Angels, controlling Copenhagen's drug trade. Defunct after 1988.

descriptions: Black Sheep MC is listed as a rival of Bullshit MC in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Hells Angels MC Morticians MC, Black Sheep MC, Galloping Goose MC (Denmark)

descriptions: Bullshit MC was formed by a merger of Filthy Few and Nøragersmindebanden.
edges.evidence_quote: Bullshit MC formed during the late 1970s in Tårnby, Amager as the result of a merger between the Filthy Few and Nøragersmindebanden

descriptions: Bullshit MC fought a violent turf war against Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: They were best known for engaging in a violent turf war against the rivaling Hells Angels Motorcycle Club.

Friends Stand United.description: Anti-fascist, anti-racist, and anti-drug hardcore crew founded in the late 1980s by Elgin James in Boston, Massachusetts. Evolved from the straight edge subculture, initially fighting Neo-Nazis in punk concerts. Later accused of unprovoked violence and intimidation, classified by the FBI as a street gang.

Hellbanianz.description: Hellbanianz is an organised street gang based in the Gascoigne Estate in Barking, London. It is involved in drug trafficking and armed robbery, with ties to the Albanian mafia. The gang gained notoriety in 2017 by flaunting wealth and weapons on social media. Its most prominent member, Tristen Asllani, was sentenced to 25 years in prison in 2016 for drug and firearms offenses.

descriptions: Hellbanianz is allied with the Albanian mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Albanian mafia

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Kinahan Cartel , A-team , Huyton Firm

Pruszków mafia.description: Polish criminal organization originating from Pruszków in the 1990s, active in the Warsaw area. Involved in car theft, drug trafficking, kidnapping, extortion, weapon trafficking, and murder. Severely weakened after 2000 when a key member became a crown witness, and largely defunct since.

descriptions: The Mokotów group splintered off from the Pruszków mafia and acted independently.
edges.evidence_quote: a group originating from the Pruszków mafia, known as the Mokotów group, splintered off and continued to act independently until 2014.

descriptions: The infobox lists Wołomin mafia as a rival of Pruszków mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Wołomin mafia

Coffin Cheaters.description: International outlaw motorcycle club formed in Perth, Australia in 1970. Expanded internationally by patching over Norwegian gangs. Known for involvement in violent conflicts, including the 1989 expulsion of the Mongrel Mob from Perth. Still active.

descriptions: Coffin Cheaters are allied with Hells Angels against Bandidos.
edges.evidence_quote: the Hells Angels allied with the Coffin Cheaters on the other

descriptions: City Crew MC is a feeder club for the US Coffin Cheaters MC.
edges.evidence_quote: City Crew Mc is a feeder club.

descriptions: Bikie war between Coffin Cheaters and Club Deroes in 1998.
edges.evidence_quote: Marc Chabriere, who was a Coffin Cheaters member murdered during the 1998 bikie war between the Coffin Cheaters and the Club Deroes.

descriptions: Coffin Cheaters, Club Deroes, Gypsy Jokers, and Gods Garbage cooperated to eject the Mongrel Mob from Perth in 1989.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1989 these four gangs combined forces to violently eject the New Zealand-based street gang, the Mongrel Mob which was attempting to establish itself in Perth.

descriptions: Coffin Cheaters absorbed Forbidden Few MC through patching over in 2004.
edges.evidence_quote: later 'patched over' two Norwegian gangs, Forbidden Few MC and Wizard MC, in 2004 and 2005 respectively.

Greco Mafia clan.description: Historically one of the most influential Mafia clans in Sicily, based in Ciaculli and Croceverde Giardini near Palermo. Founded in the late 19th century, the family played a key role in the Sicilian Cosa Nostra, with members serving as secretaries of the Sicilian Mafia Commission. They were involved in heroin trafficking, cigarette smuggling, and money laundering. Despite a decline in the 2000s, they remain active with strong influence.

descriptions: Greco is listed as an allied 'ndrina.
edges.evidence_quote: 'Ndrine alleate - Greco con a capo Angelo Greco ( 1965 )

descriptions: The Greco Mafia clan was part of the Corleonesi coalition.
edges.evidence_quote: Michele Greco from the Greco Mafia clan

descriptions: Galatolo clan fought Greco clan over control of wholesale market racket.
edges.evidence_quote: The Acquasanta Mafia clan tried to muscle in on the protection racket that traditionally belonged the 'Mafia of the Gardens' — such as the Greco, Bontade and Sorci clans from the southeast of Palermo

descriptions: Greco members participated in a meeting with American Cosa Nostra in 1957, likely discussing heroin trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: They both participated at the Grand Hotel des Palmes Mafia meeting in October 1957 between prominent American and Sicilian mafiosi.

descriptions: Salvatore Greco served as first secretary of the Sicilian Mafia Commission.
edges.evidence_quote: Salvatore "Ciaschiteddu" Greco was the first ‘secretary’ of the Sicilian Mafia Commission

descriptions: The Grecos had an uneasy alliance with the Corleonesi during the Second Mafia War.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Uneasy alliance with the Corleonesi (Second Mafia War 1981-1983)

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Acquasanta Mafia clan (Palermo wholesale market war in the mid 1950s)

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: La Barbera brothers (First Mafia War in the early 1960s)

descriptions: The La Barberas and Grecos were on opposite sides of a conflict over a drug shipment, leading to murders and a war.
edges.evidence_quote: The La Barberas accused Di Pisa of embezzling the missing heroin. The Sicilian Mafia Commission sided with Di Pisa, and the La Barberas were outraged. The La Barberas murdered Di Pisa and Manzella, triggering a war.

edges.evidence_quote: The conflict erupted over an underweight shipment of heroin... between clans allied with the Grecos... and clans allied with the La Barberas.

descriptions: The Grecos cooperated with other Mafia clans in a drug shipment.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1962, mafia boss Cesare Manzella organized a drug shipment to the United States with the help of two Sicilian clans, the Grecos and the La Barberas.

Toa-kai.description: The Toa-kai is a yakuza syndicate based in Tokyo, Japan, founded in 1948 by Hisayuki Machii as the Tosei-kai. Originally a far-right anti-communist group, it became one of Tokyo's most powerful gangs during post-war economic growth. The syndicate is a member of the Kanto Hatsuka-kai federation and aligned with the Yamaguchi-gumi. As of the infobox, it has an estimated 20 members and is led by Yasushi Hayano.

descriptions: Matsuba-kai and Toa-kai are both members of the Kantō Hatsuka-kai federation.
edges.evidence_quote: The Matsuba-kai is a member of a yakuza fraternal federation named the Kantō Hatsuka-kai, along with four other Kantō -based yakuza syndicates, the Sumiyoshi-kai , the Inagawa-kai , the Toa-kai , and the Soai-kai .

descriptions: Dojin-kai attacked Toa-kai after Toa-kai refused to cooperate in drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: the Dojin-kai attempted to compel the Toa-kai to co-operate in drug trafficking but the Toa-kai rejected it and then the mass attacks started

descriptions: Dojin-kai attempted to expand into Okinawa with heavy violence, contrasting with Toa-kai's gentle stance.
edges.evidence_quote: The reason why the Toa-kai has been able to be active on the island may be because the group has historically been more oriented to legitimate businesses, and of its gentle stance, in contrast to other yakuza syndicates which have attempted to expand their influences into Okinawa with heavy violence, such as the Yamaguchi-gumi and especially the Dojin-kai .

edges.target: General Association of Korean Residents in Japan
descriptions: The Tosei-kai (predecessor of Toa-kai) was in conflict with the General Association of Korean Residents in Japan.
edges.evidence_quote: The Tosei-kai was originally a reported far-right organization of anti-communist activism led by Machii as a sympathizer of Kanji Ishiwara, which was in conflict with the North Korea -associated General Association of Korean Residents in Japan.

descriptions: Toa-kai and Inagawa-kai are fellow members of the Kanto Hatsuka-kai federation.
edges.evidence_quote: The Toa-kai is a member of a bakuto fraternal federation named the Kanto Hatsuka-kai, along with four other Kanto -based yakuza syndicates, the Sumiyoshi-kai , the Inagawa-kai , the Matsuba-kai , and the Soai-kai .

descriptions: Toa-kai is a member of the Kanto Hatsuka-kai federation.
edges.evidence_quote: The Toa-kai is a member of a bakuto fraternal federation named the Kanto Hatsuka-kai, along with four other Kanto -based yakuza syndicates, the Sumiyoshi-kai , the Inagawa-kai , the Matsuba-kai , and the Soai-kai .

descriptions: Kudo-kai was suspected of attacking Toa-kai members.
edges.evidence_quote: it was strongly believed that the perpetrators, from the underworld, were those belonging to the Kudo-kai (controlling the Kokura Port) or the Dojin-kai

descriptions: Kyokuryu-kai has attacked and expelled other syndicates, but Toa-kai has been able to be active due to its gentle stance.
edges.evidence_quote: Okinawa's underworld has been known for its exclusiveness since the 20th century, where the dominating Kyokuryu-kai has persistently attacked and violently expelled any yakuza syndicate attempting to enter the island.

descriptions: Makoto-kai became an affiliate of Tosei-kai (predecessor of Toa-kai) in Okinawa.
edges.evidence_quote: Gibo met with Machii through Kodama, founding an affiliate of the Tosei-kai in Okinawa.

descriptions: Toa-kai and Matsuba-kai are fellow members of the Kanto Hatsuka-kai federation.
edges.evidence_quote: The Toa-kai is a member of a bakuto fraternal federation named the Kanto Hatsuka-kai, along with four other Kanto -based yakuza syndicates, the Sumiyoshi-kai , the Inagawa-kai , the Matsuba-kai , and the Soai-kai .

descriptions: Toa-kai and Soai-kai are fellow members of the Kanto Hatsuka-kai federation.
edges.evidence_quote: The Toa-kai is a member of a bakuto fraternal federation named the Kanto Hatsuka-kai, along with four other Kanto -based yakuza syndicates, the Sumiyoshi-kai , the Inagawa-kai , the Matsuba-kai , and the Soai-kai .

descriptions: Toa-kai and Sumiyoshi-kai are fellow members of the Kanto Hatsuka-kai federation.
edges.evidence_quote: The Toa-kai is a member of a bakuto fraternal federation named the Kanto Hatsuka-kai, along with four other Kanto -based yakuza syndicates, the Sumiyoshi-kai , the Inagawa-kai , the Matsuba-kai , and the Soai-kai .

descriptions: Tosei-kai and Yamaguchi-gumi made a deal allowing Yamaguchi-gumi to operate in Tokyo.
edges.evidence_quote: even the Yamaguchi-gumi 's Taoka had to cut a deal with Machii to allow that group to operate in Tokyo.

descriptions: Toa-kai has aligned with and been supported by Yamaguchi-gumi since the Taoka era and since 2005.
time_periods: since the Taoka era in the 20th century; since 2005
edges.evidence_quote: The Toa-kai has aligned itself with the largest known Yamaguchi-gumi syndicate since the syndicate's Taoka era in the 20th century, and has been closely supported by Shinobu Tsukasa , the sixth-generation godfather of the Yamaguchi-gumi, since 2005 when the sixth era of the Yamaguchi-gumi officially started.

descriptions: Yoshimi-kogyo is a branch organization of Toa-kai.
edges.evidence_quote: The Toa-kai's notable branch organizations include the Yoshimi-kogyo ( 誼興業 ) .

descriptions: Toa-kai is listed as an ally of Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Inagawa-kai, Soai-kai, Matsuba-kai, Kyōsei-kai, Aizukotetsu-kai, Toa-kai, Fukuhaku-kai, Gōda-ikka, Shinwa-kai

descriptions: Rowdy Ones is a puppet club of Hells Angels in Sorel.
edges.evidence_quote: Every Quebec region had its own puppet club: the Rockers Motor Club in Montreal, the Rowdy Ones in Sorel, the Evil Ones in Drummondville, the Satan's Guard in the Saguenay region, and the Jokers in St-Jean

descriptions: Plug Uglies and Schuykill Rangers both participated in the 1863 Draft Riots.
edges.evidence_quote: the Philadelphia Schuykill Rangers under Jimmy Haggerty and other rowdies of Philadelphia, had come to New York to participate in the riots

edges.target: General Association of Korean Residents in Japan
descriptions: The Tosei-kai (predecessor of Toa-kai) was in conflict with the General Association of Korean Residents in Japan.
edges.evidence_quote: The Tosei-kai was originally a reported far-right organization of anti-communist activism led by Machii as a sympathizer of Kanji Ishiwara, which was in conflict with the North Korea -associated General Association of Korean Residents in Japan.
